title: monitoring — PHP attribute contracts
description: Three class-level attributes. `#[AsMonitoringListener]` subscribes a service to monitoring-lane events (alert lifecycle + incident lifecycle + circuit-breaker + provider lifecycle). `#[AsMonitoringProvider]` marks a class as a provider driver — the MonitoringProviderManager discovers these at boot and registers them via createXxxDriver factories. `#[AsHealthCheckStrategy]` marks a class as a health check executor strategy — the HealthCheckExecutor discovers these + dispatches per check_type.

attributes.name: AsMonitoringListener
attributes.description: Marks a service as a subscriber to monitoring-lane events (MonitoringAlertFired / Acknowledged / Resolved / Suppressed, MonitoringIncidentOpened / Acknowledged / StatusUpdated / Resolved, MonitoringProviderCircuitBreakerOpened / Closed, MonitoringProviderConfigured / Deactivated, HealthCheck* variants). Discovery loader picks up each class at boot + wires the declared events. Consumers of MonitoringAlertFired for P1 should override queue to `monitoring-critical`.

params.name: events
params.description: Array of event short names (`MonitoringAlertFired`, `MonitoringIncidentOpened`, `MonitoringProviderCircuitBreakerOpened`, …). Empty = subscribe to every `Academorix\Observability\Monitoring\Events\*` event. Names resolve against events.json at boot; typos abort boot with a discovery error.

params.name: queue
params.description: Optional queue override. Falls back to the event's default queue (see events.json).

params.name: priority
params.description: Handler ordering within a single event. Lower runs first. Failover listeners (FailoverToAlternateProvider) declare priority 10 so they run before default notification listeners.

attributes.name: AsMonitoringProvider
attributes.description: Marks a class as a monitoring provider driver. The MonitoringProviderManager discovers these at boot and registers a createXxxDriver factory keyed on the slug. Every driver MUST implement the IMonitoringProvider contract (name(), supports(MonitoringSeverity), notify(...), transform(...)) + ship a JSON Schema under data/providers/<slug>-config.schema.json for config validation.

params.name: slug
params.description: Provider slug matching the MonitoringProvider enum (sentry, datadog, pagerduty, opsgenie, prometheus, custom_webhook). One driver class per slug.

params.name: supports_severities
params.description: MonitoringSeverity values this provider can accept: subset of {p1, p2, p3, p4}. All 6 day-1 providers support all 4 severities; the routing decision is per-config (enabled_severities on the provider_config).

params.name: is_paging
params.description: true = the provider drives real-time paging (PagerDuty, Opsgenie). Used by MonitoringPagingCoordinator to route P1 alerts preferentially to paging providers.

params.name: is_apm
params.description: true = the provider is an APM / observability platform (Sentry, Datadog). Used for correlation labels.

params.name: supports_test_mode
params.description: true = the provider has a distinct sandbox / test endpoint. Every day-1 provider supports test mode.

attributes.name: AsHealthCheckStrategy
attributes.description: Marks a class as a health check executor strategy. The HealthCheckExecutor discovers these at boot + dispatches per check_type. Every strategy MUST implement the IHealthCheckStrategy contract (checkType(), execute(HealthCheck): HealthCheckRunResult).

params.name: check_type
params.description: HealthCheckType value this strategy handles (db_ping / redis_ping / queue_depth / memory_usage / cpu_usage / disk_usage / http_endpoint / dns_lookup / tls_expiry / custom_script). One strategy class per check_type.
